title: Weekly comment: Is political volatility more dangerous than market volatility?
section_path: Weekly comment: Is political volatility more dangerous than market volatility?
section_path_text: Weekly comment: Is political volatility more dangerous than market volatility?
summary:
Main
By George Lagarias
Born in September 63 BCE, Gaius Octavius Thurinus, was a noble Roman, ushered into an age of trouble. In the very days when he was welcomed into the world, Rome was being plunged into a violent civil war as a nobleman, Catilina, tried to overthrow the Senate. Still, the Republic was saved, peace ensued and young Octavius's life was being planned for him.
